name: Maltese Sauce (Malteser Sosse)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small skillet, simmer green onion in wine, vinegar, and chicken stock for 5 minutes over low heat. Strain well and discard the green onion.
2. Place egg yolks and wine mixture in a small enamel-lined saucepan. Use a double boiler or place over hot water. The water should barely be simmering at all times.
3. Add butter one piece at a time, mixing well after each addition until fully incorporated. You can use a whisk to help incorporate the butter into the yolk mixture.
4. When all the butter has been added, the sauce should be thick and creamy. Stir in lemon juice, salt, white pepper, and any remaining orange peel shavings.
5. Gently fold in the whipped cream just before serving. Serve immediately to prevent the sauce from separating.
6. This sauce is delicious with delicate fish or vegetable dishes, such as asparagus, and pairs well with broiled meat. Makes 4 servings.
